name: disag-md
description: Disag-MD — Python hydrology tools + SvelteKit web UI on AWS.

scripts.//-- setup --: One-time Python venv for the local Lambda shim. Idempotent.
scripts.setup:py: python3 -m venv web/backend/.venv && web/backend/.venv/bin/pip install --quiet --upgrade pip && web/backend/.venv/bin/pip install --quiet -r web/backend/requirements-dev.txt
scripts.//-- dev --: Two-process dev loop: SvelteKit on :5173, local Lambda shim on :8000.
scripts.dev:api: test -x web/backend/.venv/bin/python || pnpm setup:py; LOCAL_S3=${LOCAL_S3:-1} web/backend/.venv/bin/python web/backend/local_server.py
scripts.//-- build --: Build the SvelteKit static site and the Lambda zip.
scripts.check: pnpm check:web && pnpm check:py && pnpm check:tf
scripts.check:web: pnpm --filter disag-md-web check && pnpm --filter disag-md-web lint && pnpm --filter disag-md-web test:unit
scripts.check:tf: cd web/infra && terraform fmt -check -recursive && terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate
scripts.format: pnpm --filter disag-md-web format && cd web/infra && terraform fmt -recursive
scripts.//-- e2e --: Playwright tests live in web/frontend/e2e/. Mocked specs run with `pnpm e2e` (no backend needed). Integration specs in e2e/integration/ run against a real local backend with `pnpm e2e:integration` — requires `pnpm setup` so the Python venv exists.
scripts.//-- infra --: Terraform lifecycle. Local apply uses AWS SSO — run `pnpm tf:login` first. backend.config holds the tfstate bucket name (gitignored — see web/infra/backend.config.example).
scripts.tf:login: echo 'Run: aws sso login --profile $AWS_PROFILE' && [ -n "$AWS_PROFILE" ] && aws sso login --profile "$AWS_PROFILE" || echo 'Set AWS_PROFILE to your SSO profile and re-run.'
scripts.//-- sops --: Prod secrets live ENCRYPTED in the private ../infra-secrets repo (disag/prod.sops.yaml), NOT here — this repo is public. Edit/rotate from that repo: `sops disag/prod.sops.yaml`. Terraform reads the sibling file via the carlpett/sops provider; decryption needs the SSO-active KMS principal — `pnpm tf:login` first.
scripts.//-- deploy --: Local one-shot deploy. CI handles release-gated deploys via .github/workflows/deploy.yml. deploy:api uses `update-function-code` because lambda.tf has `ignore_changes = [filename, source_code_hash]` (to avoid drift from CI-pushed code), so `tf:apply` rebuilds the zip but never uploads it.
scripts.deploy: pnpm build && pnpm tf:apply && pnpm deploy:api && pnpm deploy:web
scripts.deploy:api: cd web/infra && aws lambda update-function-code --function-name "$(terraform output -raw lambda_function_name)" --zip-file "fileb://$(pwd)/../backend/lambda.zip" --publish > /dev/null && echo 'Lambda code updated.'
scripts.deploy:web: cd web/infra && aws s3 sync ../frontend/build/ "s3://$(terraform output -raw frontend_bucket)/" --delete && aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id "$(terraform output -raw cloudfront_distribution_id)" --paths '/*'

//-- overrides --: pnpm overrides live in pnpm-workspace.yaml for v10+ workspaces.
